title: Regional Jet Market
description: Analyze the regional jet market using provided capacity, demand, and cost data.
additional_info: Exhibit #1: Capacity (# of jets) for Bombardier, Embraer, R, and C from 2013 to 2017.
Exhibit #2: Projected Annual Demand (# of jets) for Bombardier, Embraer, R, and C from 2013 to 2017.
Exhibit #3: Cost Breakdown ($B) at full capacity for Bombardier, Embraer, R, and C.
solution: Not explicitly provided in the text.
evaluation: Not explicitly provided in the text.

title: BankCo Online Banking
description: BankCoCard wants to know if they should start an online bank and if so, should they launch a savings or a checking account for their new online bank.
additional_info: BankCoCard wants to launch both a savings and checking account, but budget allows only one product this year.
Savings and checking accounts cost $10 million each to build and take the same time.
Current cost of funds is 4% for the credit card portfolio; interest rates stable.
Assume all deposits can be lent, no reserves required.
Online bank will operate only within the US.
The online bank will lend deposits to the credit card portfolio at 3% interest.
Savings account average deposit: $5,000; checking account average deposit: $2,000.
Savings account lifetime profit calculation: $110 per account; checking account lifetime profit: $280 per account.
Market capture expected at 10% of online accounts in first year.
Market size and growth data provided for online savings and checking accounts.
Risks include market capture less than 10%, reputation risk, acquisition cost, product differentiation, regulatory risk.
solution: BankCoCard should start an online bank, launching the checking account first because it generates almost twice as much lifetime profit and the market for online checking accounts is growing faster.
evaluation: Candidate should design a framework, brainstorm product launch, revenues & costs, account acquisition, calculate account-level profitability and market size, and consider risks and next steps.

title: Made to Measure
description: Our client owns a designer shoe-store making high-end made-to-measure shoes. They want to increase output without affecting quality despite limited skilled labor.
additional_info: No additional skilled shoemakers available.
Shoe-making process has 3 stages: cutting, stitching, finishing.
Currently 15 workers, each takes 12 hours to produce one pair of shoes performing all steps.
Different shoemakers have different skill sets; specialization possible.
Employees work 9 hours/day, 5 days/week.
Price per pair: $300; material cost $40; shipping $10; wages $15/hour; fixed costs $1000/week.
solution: Change the process so each group of employees specializes in a specific task, reducing average time per pair from 12 to 9 hours, increasing output and weekly profit.
evaluation: Candidate should explore the process, identify misalignment of skills and tasks, propose specialization, and calculate new output and profit.

title: Sunshine Power
description: Sunshine Power owns a coal power station and piloted SunSteam solar tech to reduce coal consumption. They consider expanding with four more arrays but need to evaluate if the investment meets payback criteria.
additional_info: Cost of one SunSteam array: $3.5M AUD; expansion cost for four arrays: $14M AUD.
One array saves 0.2% of annual coal consumption; four arrays save 0.8%.
Annual coal consumption: 4 million metric tons.
SunSteam operating costs assumed zero.
Client requires payback in less than 5 years based on cost savings.
Break-even coal price for payback: $87.50 per ton.
Current coal price approx. $83 per ton.
Risks include coal price volatility and technical difficulties.
solution: Based on current coal prices, payback is not possible. If coal prices rise above $87.50, payback criteria will be met. Recommend hedging coal price risk and negotiating construction costs.
evaluation: Candidate should calculate fixed investment, coal savings, break-even coal price, and assess risks and benefits.

title: Beijing International Airport
description: The CEO of Beijing Capital International Airport asks for advice on whether Beijing Airport should consider acquiring smaller airports in light of Shanghai Airport Authority's acquisition plans.
additional_info: Shanghai Airport interested in acquiring Weihai and Tai’an airports in Shandong Province.
No financial details on acquisition prices.
Beijing Airport has no specific acquisition targets.
Airport industry revenue concentration by region provided.
Revenue streams: aircraft movement & service charges (50%+), concessions, airport fees, other services, parking fees.
Costs mostly fixed: equipment, wages, depreciation, rent & utilities.
No significant synergies expected from acquiring smaller airports.
Airports are fixed-cost businesses with limited synergy potential.
additional_questions: Who is the actual client in this scenario?
What is the Beijing Airport CEO's main concern or interest?
Should Beijing Airport develop an M&A strategy?
solution: Recommend against developing an M&A strategy due to lack of synergies and benefits. Suggest exploring other revenue/profit boosting strategies.
evaluation: Candidate should identify client perspective, analyze industry economics, assess synergies, and provide strategic recommendation.

title: Grad-U-Date
description: Grad-U-Date, an online dating startup for graduate students, seeks advice on the best monetization strategy and profitability.
additional_info: Gender ratio 50/50; operates only in the US.
Four major competitors and many small ones; no other graduate student focused dating services.
Website already built (sunk cost).
Only one revenue model can be used.
Fixed costs: $10 million/year; variable cost: $1 per member/year.
Market size approx. 6 million graduate students.
Revenue models: monthly fee, premium account, advertising.
Market trends show decline in monthly fee paying users since 2013.
Risks include decreasing paying users, competition, affordability, and market size changes.
additional_questions: Does new market info change your revenue model recommendation?
Why did monthly fee paying users drop starting 2013?
What strategies can increase profit given your recommended model?
solution: Recommend premium account revenue model with expected $23.3M annual profit. Monthly fee model is declining due to competition.
evaluation: Candidate should brainstorm monetization models, perform market sizing, calculate profitability, analyze market trends, and propose profit improvement strategies.

title: A Sticky Paper Situation
description: PaperCo, a specialty paper manufacturer, wants to invest to restore growth. Identify the best investment opportunity among customer segments.
additional_info: Products sold to 24,000 commercial printers in US: 20,000 small, 3,000 medium, 1,000 large.
Market shares: 30% small, 10% medium and large.
Potential to increase sales by 20% in medium or large segments with production expansion.
Different packaging preferences by segment: small (boxes), medium (cartons), large (pallets).
Expansion costs: $675K for medium segment, $1.3M for large segment.
Unit prices and costs vary by segment; profit per unit: $10 small, $9 medium, $7 large.
Industry mature with low growth; no significant regulatory or competitive trends.
ROI calculated for 1-year horizon shows medium segment has higher ROI.
solution: Recommend expanding production in medium printer segment due to higher 1-year ROI and lower expansion cost; consider large segment later.
evaluation: Candidate should calculate profitability per segment, ROI, and compare investment costs and returns.

title: New Bar Profitability
description: An entrepreneur wants to invest in a new bar and needs to determine profitability and convince an investor of viability.
additional_info: Consider factors such as fixed and variable costs, pricing, customer volume, competition, location, and market demand.
solution: Not fully provided in the text; candidate expected to investigate key profitability drivers and perform calculations.
evaluation: Candidate should identify relevant cost and revenue drivers, structure calculations, and communicate assumptions clearly.

title: Botox
description: Your client is considering launching Botox as a prophylactic injection for migraines in the US market. Estimate the market size, assess the considerations for launching Botox in this new indication, and provide a recommendation on whether and how to proceed.
additional_info: The drug will be priced at $200 per injection.
Remaining development and launch costs amount to approximately $5B.
Manufacturing marginal costs are expected to be quite small.
Annual marketing expense can be estimated at $1B.
The product is given every 2 months (6 injections per year).
Offers similar efficacy compared to market leading option but without any side effects.
When taken for migraines it has some added skin care benefits.
US population is 300 million.
20% of the population suffers from chronic migraines.
50% of chronic migraine sufferers experience severe migraines and are willing to purchase prescription products.
Assume 20%-40% market share for Botox in 5 years.
The market leading branded prescription oral migraine treatment lost patent protection last year.
Strong over-the-counter generic market for migraine treatments.
solution: The potential annual Botox market size is estimated between $7B and $14B. While the market is promising with large revenue and profit potential, significant challenges exist including brand image risks, regulatory hurdles, potential cannibalization of the cosmetic Botox segment, pricing strategy, and insurance coverage. Launching under a different brand name is recommended to differentiate the products and manage risks. The client should continue clinical trials, set up regulatory and marketing barriers between markets, and secure insurance contracts.
evaluation: Evaluate candidate's ability to perform market sizing accurately, identify and analyze launch considerations (branding, regulatory, pricing, competition), and provide a balanced recommendation with risks and next steps.

title: Bike Helmets
description: Your client is a private equity firm considering purchasing a US-based company that manufactures bicycle helmets and accessories. Determine whether the firm should purchase this company by analyzing expected demand, market size, and projected profits.
additional_info: The company sells only in the US.
Revenue split evenly between helmets and accessories.
Company holds 70% market share across both product lines.
Company shifted from high-end to mass market.
High-end market is 40% of total, mass market 60%.
Most new entrants are in the high-end market.
Assume US population 320 million.
75% of population rides bikes.
75% of bike riders wear helmets.
Average helmets bought per year per rider is 0.5.
Price per helmet is $40.
solution: The total annual market size is approximately $7.2B, with the company generating about $5.1B in revenue given its 70% market share. The company is moving towards a higher margin, less competitive, and growing mass market segment, making it an attractive investment. Risks include sustaining market share and competition from private label brands. The investment attractiveness depends heavily on the purchase price and market assumptions.
evaluation: Assess candidate's ability to structure market sizing, justify assumptions, analyze market dynamics, and provide a reasoned investment recommendation.

title: Skin Care Market (Deloitte)
description: Our client is a $5B European manufacturer of medicinal products considering entering the European consumer skin care market. Analyze the attractiveness of this market, advise on channel and product strategy, and recommend whether to enter Europe or other geographic markets.
additional_info: Global skin care market is $30B with 7% CAGR.
Branded market is $20B (10% CAGR), generic $10B (12% CAGR).
OTC market is $15B (8% CAGR), prescription $10B (8% CAGR).
Europe accounts for 20% of global market with 5% growth.
US accounts for 40% with 10% growth.
Japan has -2% growth with high barriers to entry.
Emerging markets have 30% growth but low margins.
Client has manufacturing in urban locations with higher overhead but commands price premium.
Client owns 10% of European medicinal market.
Client aims to double top line in 5 years.
additional_questions: What factors should they consider in deciding whether to enter the European Consumer Skin Care market?
How would you advise the client in developing a European channel strategy (Prescription, OTC, or Both?) and product strategy (Branded or Generic)?
What factors should drive their decision to explore other geographic markets versus staying in Europe?
Do you think expansion into this market is a good idea given the limited information?
solution: The European skin care market is moderately attractive but small relative to the client's growth target. The client should consider market fragmentation, growth rates, basis of competition, and alignment with existing capabilities. OTC channels and branded products may better support growth, but manufacturing costs and pricing pressures are concerns. Expanding beyond Europe to larger and faster-growing markets like the US or emerging markets may be necessary to meet growth targets. The decision should weigh trade-offs between margin, growth, and strategic fit.
evaluation: Evaluate candidate's ability to analyze market attractiveness, channel and product strategy, geographic considerations, and to synthesize a recommendation balancing growth and profitability.

title: Great Burger (McKinsey)
description: Great Burger (GB), a fast food chain, is considering acquiring Heavenly Donuts (HD), a doughnut producer. Advise GB on whether to acquire HD by analyzing market growth, synergies, profitability, and integration challenges.
additional_info: GB operates 5,000 stores globally; HD operates 1,020 stores.
GB has 85% franchised stores; HD franchises regions with store opening requirements.
GB sales/store: $1.1M; HD sales/store: $0.7M.
GB profit margin: 6.3%; HD profit margin: 4.9%.
US doughnut consumption per capita is $10/year, projected to grow to $20/year in 5 years.
US population assumed 300 million.
Potential synergies include SG&A cost savings, purchasing scale, cross-selling products, international expansion, and better site selection.
Cannibalization of GB sales by HD products estimated at 10%.
Incremental profit per GB store from selling HD doughnuts estimated at $15K.
additional_questions: What areas would you want to explore to determine whether GB should acquire HD?
What potential synergies can you think of between GB and HD?
What sales/store will HD require in 5 years to double US market share and expand stores 2.5x?
How would you assess the profitability impact of selling doughnuts in GB stores?
What would be the incremental profit per store given sales, margin, and cannibalization assumptions?
Summarize McKinsey’s perspective on whether GB should acquire HD.
solution: Acquiring HD could create significant value through cost synergies (corporate SG&A), revenue synergies (cross-selling, international expansion), and operational improvements (store site selection). To meet growth targets, HD needs to increase sales/store to $1.2M with expanded store count. Selling HD doughnuts in GB stores yields incremental profits despite cannibalization. Overall, acquisition is favorable but requires detailed synergy quantification and integration planning.
evaluation: Assess candidate’s ability to identify key due diligence areas, quantify synergies, perform sales/store calculations, analyze profitability impacts including cannibalization, and synthesize a clear recommendation.

title: Cosmetics Company (Capgemini)
description: You are asked to present to the board a turnaround plan for a North American personal and home care products company currently operating at a loss. Analyze internal and external factors, assess market and customer data, and recommend strategies to return the business to profitability within two years.
additional_info: Revenue streams include existing and new products.
Cost drivers include R&D, manufacturing, and operations.
Competitors vary by customer segments; industry rivalry intensity varies.
Products are sold through various retail channels with differing power.
Women represent the majority of consumption; growth driven by female Baby Boomers and 55+ males.
Consumer spending correlates with perception of necessity.
Competitors use diverse or dedicated channel strategies.
Client currently has a misplaced channel strategy given product diversity.
Data exhibits include growth by age group, share of wallet vs. perception of need, and product-channel strategy maps.
additional_questions: Assess and analyze the provided data exhibits.
What other considerations should be analyzed before finalizing recommendations?
solution: Focus on women as primary consumers while monitoring growth in older male segments. Develop deep customer insights to increase share of wallet. Diversify distribution channels to align with product categories. Address manufacturing and supply chain considerations. Risks include market growth slowdown and changing consumer preferences. Next steps involve customer research, marketing investment, and channel diversification analysis.
evaluation: Evaluate candidate’s ability to interpret data, identify key internal and external factors, propose actionable recommendations, and consider risks and next steps.

title: Drinks Gone Flat
description: The beverage department of a regional supermarket has seen a drop in revenue over the last year and has hired your firm to determine the cause of the decline and recommend ways to reverse the trend.
additional_info: Client is a leading regional grocer within the southeastern U.S.
Competitive landscape has not changed in the last year
Revenue decline is specifically within the beverage segment
Client wants to find the cause of the declining revenue and recommendations for how to stop the decline
Revenue / sales numbers segmented by beverage product category (Exhibit 1)
Further segmented information by soda brand (Exhibit 2)
additional_questions: The client would like to hear your recommendations for how to reverse the decline in revenue of the beverage segment.
solution: The decline in beverage revenue is due to a decline in sodas, specifically Brand A's volume decline and a large increase in volume for the Value Brand with a price drop. Recommendations include adjusting the price of the Value Brand, improving differentiation from Brand A and Value Brand, exploring pricing strategies, bundling opportunities, marketing activities, and shelf placement.
evaluation: Candidate should focus on revenue levers (price and quantity), avoid cost discussions, use a structured framework (e.g., 4P's), identify the root cause (Value Brand price drop), and provide creative, structured recommendations to reverse the decline.

title: Made to Measure
description: Our client owns a designer shoe-store that makes high end made-to-measure shoes for customers. The high-quality product has gained popularity rapidly and the client wants to ramp up production. However, the highly-skilled labor that our client employs is hard to come by. Our client wants us to help them figure out how to increase output without adversely affecting quality and the company’s potential profitability.
additional_info: There are no additional skilled shoemakers available in the market
The shoe-making process has 3 stages: cutting, stitching and finishing
Currently there are 15 workers, each taking an average of 12 hours to produce one pair of shoes
Each shoemaker performs all three steps under the current system
Process can be changed so shoemakers perform specific tasks
Employee survey shows hours per activity by group
Employees work 9 hours a day, 5 days a week
Price: $300/pair, Material cost: $40/pair, Shipping: $10/pair, Employee wages: $15/hour, Fixed costs: $1000/week
solution: By reorganizing the process so each group specializes in the task they perform fastest, the average time per pair reduces from 12 to 9 hours, increasing weekly output from 56 to 75 pairs. Profitability improves with weekly profit estimated at $7,625 and annual profit at $396,500.
evaluation: Candidate should identify misalignment of skills and tasks, propose specialization, calculate new output and profitability, and consider risks and implementation challenges.

title: Vibrants
description: Your client, a national drug-store, is adding a new type of hair dye to its retail offering. “Highlights Dye” allows customers to comb in small amounts of various color to hair rather than the traditional method of dying the whole head. The client is considering launching its own in-house brand or carrying an existing recognized brand-name – “Vibrants”. Should the drugstore carry Vibrants over the in-store brand?
additional_info: The retail chain already carries their in-house brand in many products including traditional hair dye
“Highlights Dye” is a fashion-forward product available in natural and fluorescent colors, lasting roughly 30 days
Target customer is under 30 years of age
Business objective is profitability
Timeline is 90-days to launch
In-house brand has superior margins but target customers under 30 prefer Vibrants
If selling Vibrants, the drugstore cannot change retail price or run promotions
Market size is approximately $750,000
solution: The drug-store should carry Vibrants despite the in-house brand having superior margins because the target customer segment (under 30) overwhelmingly prefers Vibrants. Launching Vibrants allows meeting customer preferences and faster time to market.
evaluation: Candidate should analyze customer preferences, margins, launch timelines, and risks such as trend volatility and pricing flexibility.

title: Sports Bar
description: Your client is an entrepreneur looking to invest in a new bar. He needs to determine how profitable the company will be and convince his primary investor, his father, that it will be a viable business. What factors would you consider and investigate?
additional_info: Average customer spends $15 on food and $20 on drinks per visit
Lease cost is $10,000 per month
Labor costs: Kitchen (4 people @ $10/hr), Bar (1 person @ $5/hr), Wait Staff (3 people @ $5/hr)
Food has a 12% gross margin, Drinks have a 50% gross margin
No specific data on utilities, legal, insurance, licenses, training, remodeling, equipment, and other startup costs
Customer traffic varies by day and time (Exhibit 1)
Start-up capital needed is $500K
Break-even and ROI calculations assume no interest
additional_questions: Your client estimates that the start-up capital needed to open the Sports Bar is $500K. How long will it take for the business to break even? Over a 5-yr period, what would be the return on this investment? (Assume no interest)
solution: The business is profitable with an annual profit of approximately $104,050, but the break-even point is about 4.8 years and 5-year ROI is 4.1%, which is low. The client should compare this investment to other opportunities and consider additional costs and revenue streams.
evaluation: Candidate should structure profitability analysis clearly, calculate revenues, costs, labor, and lease, estimate break-even and ROI, and discuss risks and recommendations.

title: The Pricing Games
description: Your client is Next Level Gaming (NLG), a start-up in the E-sports and computer gaming industry based in Los Angeles, California. NLG is planning to launch its first game – an online, multiplayer role playing game that is unlike any existing franchise. Being a new player in the industry, NLG’s CEO, Bobby Beck, has asked for your help in deciding its business model. The company is considering 3 alternatives: a subscription model where players pay a monthly fee; a retail model where players pay full price at initial purchase, and a free-to-play model where the game is free to play but charges players for in-game merchandise. How would you advise NLG to proceed?
additional_info: Time frame: NLG is looking to launch immediately
Competition: The online gaming industry is dominated by 3 major players with 35%, 25%, and 20% market share
Target Market: Majority of players are between ages 21 to 40
Geography: US only
Platform: Compatible with Apple and Windows computers
R&D Cost: $10 million (sunk cost)
Market sizing estimate: approximately 10 million players
Profitability calculations for each business model available in Exhibits 1 & 2
additional_questions: What factors would you consider to estimate the number of players NLG can target with its first game? How large is this player base in the US?
What is NLG’s expected first year profitability with each of the 3 pricing models?
solution: Market size is estimated at 10 million players in the US. Profitability calculations show subscription model yields $20M profit (16.67% margin), retail model $9M profit (12.5% margin), and free-to-play $20M profit (13.33% margin). Qualitative factors such as customer acquisition costs, retention, competitive response, and expansion potential should guide the choice. Subscription and free-to-play models offer higher profits and scalability.
evaluation: Candidate should structure market sizing, perform profitability calculations for each model, consider qualitative factors, and recommend an optimal business model based on financial and strategic considerations.

title: The Pricing Games
description: NLG’s CEO, Bobby Beck, wants to meet for lunch to discuss your findings. How would you summarize your recommendation?
additional_info: Subscription, Retail, and Free-to-Play business models with target audience of 10M each.
Predicted market shares: Subscription 10%, Retail 12%, Free-to-Play 50%.
Monthly subscription fees: $10 for Subscription; Retail price $60; Free-to-Play has annual in-game purchases of $100.
Annual variable costs, customer acquisition costs, and fixed costs differ by model.
Survey responses from beta test on customer preferences for each model.
solution: Recommend pursuing the free-to-play business model due to largest player base and long-term competitive positioning. Recognize risks in generating paying players and suggest in-game purchases to mitigate.
evaluation: Candidate should show consistency with brainstorm, cite relevant insights, mention profit margins, recognize risks, and suggest mitigation methods.

title: Men’s Extra Comfortable Essentials
description: Our client, Men’s Extra Comfortable Essentials, is a US-based manufacturer of basic apparel including socks, tanks, tees, and underwear. Revenues in 2016 were $60M, and the CEO has promised shareholders 4x growth by 2020, at which point, she promised 10.5% profit margin. She has hired us to determine whether these are realistic revenue targets, and if so, how her firm could go about achieving them.
additional_info: Firm purchases fabrics through US suppliers and processes them in-house.
Current product mix and market shares by product line (socks, tanks, tees, underwear).
Market CAGR by product line from 2016 to 2020.
Profitability data including margins by product line.
Firm currently only produces products for men.
additional_questions: Given the data you have, what will be our client’s 2020 revenue by product type?
Given the following gross margin information what is the gross margin for the firm in 2020?
How can the firm reach the targets promised to shareholders?

The numbers make sense, but of the remaining two product lines, only socks market share can remain constant (at 15%). Underwear’s market share must increase from 1% to 10%; what are some ways the firm can attempt to do this?
solution: Recommend dropping Tanks and Tees product lines to achieve margin goals and focus on increasing market share of Underwear lines through new products or female product lines.
evaluation: Candidate should calculate market sizes and revenues, identify unprofitable lines to cut, brainstorm growth strategies for underwear, and recognize risks and next steps.

title: WiFi in the Sky
description: Your firm has won an RFP to help a domestic airline carrier examine their in-flight connectivity (IFC) strategy. With 80% of US-based aircraft already outfitted with IFC technology and competitive pressures rising, offering WiFi service is becoming table-stakes. Your client has yet to enter the game, but they know it’s something they need to consider to stay competitive. What are some of the key things the client should think about when assessing their go-to-market strategy for IFC?
additional_info: The airline flies primarily domestic routes within the continental US and select international flights.
IFC includes only WiFi connectivity; in-flight entertainment is separate but can be integrated.
The airline has 90 planes and services ~30K flights per year.
IFC is typically installed and managed by outside vendors with long-term contracts.
Different vendors use different technologies with varying quality and cost structures.
Total bandwidth is shared across passengers; pricing can affect usage and service quality.
additional_questions: There is a wide range of potential business models and your client wants to better understand them before making a vendor decision. What are your thoughts on these two models? [Exhibit 1]

After some initial analysis, the client has decided to partner with a white-label IFC vendor to outfit all 90 of its planes. What percentage of their passengers need to purchase a session for the airline to break even in 2 years? [Exhibit 2]

Market research tells us that take rates are only between 5 and 10% today for paid connectivity, so your client is concerned about being able to hit the break-even targets. What do you think are the key drivers of IFC take rate?
solution: Recommend partnering with a wholesale, white-label vendor to retain control over pricing and user experience, roll out gradually prioritizing newer planes and routes with high expected take rates.
evaluation: Candidate should consider ROI, pricing models, market demand, costs, vendor contracts, product experience, technology, and rollout strategy. Should complete break-even math and brainstorm take rate drivers.

title: MJ Wineries
description: Our client MJ Wineries produces wine in Sonoma Valley, California. MJ Wineries specializes in growing Chardonnay grapes and has won numerous awards for their famous full-bodied Chardonnay. Although MJ Wineries market share in Chardonnay remains strong, profitability has recently declined. The CEO has hired your firm to recommend ways to immediately improve profitability.
additional_info: It takes at least nine months to produce a bottle of Chardonnay; wines get better as they age.
MJ Wineries owns a large 3,000 acre vineyard and production equipment.
The wine industry is capital intensive with many small producers.
Customer segments include liquor stores, restaurants, bars/nightclubs, supermarkets.
US market share and profit margins for various wine types provided.
Customer preference is shifting from white wine to red wine.
additional_questions: What could be impacting profitability at MJ Wineries?
Which grape variety should MJ wineries consider for producing its new wine? [Exhibits 1 and 2]
What are some pros and cons for MJ Wineries to consider when moving into the Pinot Noir market?
What are some other ways MJ Wineries could increase revenue without introducing a new type of wine?
solution: Recommend expanding into producing Pinot Noir to capture greater market share and diversify risk. Also consider contingency plans such as expanding winery services or alternative revenue streams.
evaluation: Candidate should brainstorm profitability issues, analyze market trends, assess new product opportunities, and propose contingency revenue growth ideas.

title: Botox
description: The maker of Botox is considering expanding to the migraine market and has already begun clinical trials in this area. The product is expected to receive FDA approval in 2019. Your team has been hired to assess the viability of this product for launch. How will you determine if the market is attractive? If the market is attractive, do you have any recommendations regarding how to launch the product? What concerns should the client take into consideration before deciding to launch?
additional_info: Price: $200 per injection; injections given every 2 months (6 per year).
Remaining development and launch costs: $5B; annual marketing expense: $1B.
Product offers similar efficacy to market leader but without side effects; added skin care benefits.
Market leading oral migraine treatment lost patent protection; strong OTC generic market.
US population 320M; ~15% suffer chronic migraines; 25% of those have severe migraines.
Expected Botox market share ~30% within 5 years.
additional_questions: What concerns should the client take into consideration before deciding to launch?
solution: Market is attractive with large revenue potential but faces challenges including branding risks, cannibalization of cosmetic Botox, regulatory hurdles, and competition. Recommend possibly launching under a different brand name to differentiate products.
evaluation: Candidate should perform market sizing, assess launch considerations including branding, cannibalization, competition, regulatory issues, and make a clear recommendation.

title: FlashPro
description: Your client is FlashPro Inc., a San Francisco based technology company that develops and sells wearable and mountable action cameras. FlashPro’s CEO believes that the company’s growth has stagnated and has hired your firm to remedy that issue.
additional_info: Company is public and sells products in US and internationally.
Main product is CamPro, a mounted camera used in action sports and other recreational activities.
FlashPro contracts manufacturing in China.
Sales channels: Distributors, Retail, and FlashPro’s Website.
Industry is highly competitive with loyal customers.
Camera price is $250; gross margin steady at 50%.
60% of sales come from US; 40% international.
additional_questions: What is FlashPro’s expected Gross Profit for 2018? [Exhibits 1-3]
FlashPro wants to understand revenue growth prospects and alternatives to boost top line. What are your ideas?
From an economic perspective what is the most attractive country to enter and what things should FlashPro consider before entering? [Exhibit 4]
solution: Recommend pursuing geographic expansion strategy, with Argentina as the most attractive market due to higher expected revenue and favorable sales channel distribution.
evaluation: Candidate should analyze profitability, growth prospects, market share, and creatively brainstorm growth strategies including new markets and channels. Should perform math accurately and consider market entry factors.

title: Bike Helmets
description: Your client is a private equity firm considering purchasing a firm that makes bicycle helmets. You have been hired to help advise whether or not to make the acquisition.
additional_info: Target company sells only in the U.S. with 60% market share.
Company has had a string of ineffective CEOs.
Average helmet price is $30; prices expected to remain stable.
Market is mature with few new entrants.
U.S. regulations require riders under 18 to wear helmets.
PE firm holds companies for average 7 years and targets 10% annual return.
PE firm also owns a bicycle manufacturer (possible synergies unknown).
solution: Candidate should weigh factors for and against investment, consider valuation methods, market size, growth potential, synergies, and timeline for value creation. No single correct answer.
evaluation: Candidate should structure analysis, justify assumptions, consider market and company factors, and make a reasoned recommendation.

title: EnerForce Market Attractiveness and Valuation
description: EnerForce is launching a new 100 KW Turbine product. Which market (Asia, U.S., Europe) is the most attractive for EnerForce’s upcoming product in the next year? How many units could EnerForce sell in each market? Should EnerForce be acquired for $700M given plant capacities and profit margins?
additional_info: Importance of product characteristics varies by market: Asia values output and costs; U.S. values aesthetics and output; Europe values size and output.
Competitive landscape: EnerForce ranks differently across markets on durability, size, aesthetics, output, brand, and cost.
Market size and growth rates: Asia (4.5 GW, 2%), U.S. (5 GW, 9%), Europe (6 GW, -13%).
Plant capacity: China 2,000 turbines/year, Vietnam 3,000 turbines/year; turbines sold at $100K with 15% profit margin; discount rate 10%.
Market share assumptions: Asia 10%, U.S. 50%, Europe 40%.
additional_questions: Can you walk me through the three charts showing market attractiveness?
How many units would you predict EnerForce could sell in each market? Please state your assumptions.
Given plant capacities and financial assumptions, should EnerForce be acquired for $700M?
solution: The U.S. and Europe markets are most attractive based on product alignment and growth rates. EnerForce can sell approximately 4,590 turbines in Asia, 27,250 in the U.S., and 20,880 in Europe next year. With a combined plant capacity of 5,000 turbines/year and a 15% profit margin on $100K turbines, the valuation is $750M, making acquisition at $700M favorable. Risks include competitive threats from Asian competitors, tariffs, customer preferences, and technology obsolescence.
evaluation: Candidate should analyze market attractiveness using product characteristics and competitive positioning, estimate sales volumes with clear assumptions, perform DCF valuation correctly, and identify acquisition risks and next steps.

title: Sunshine Power: SunSteam Expansion Profitability
description: Sunshine Power operates a coal power station and piloted SunSteam solar technology to reduce coal consumption. Should Sunshine Power expand the pilot by constructing four additional SunSteam arrays given investment criteria requiring payback within 5 years?
additional_info: Cost per array assumed $3.5M; four arrays total $14M fixed cost.
One array saves 0.2% of annual coal consumption; four arrays save 0.8%.
Annual coal consumption is 4 million metric tons.
Operating costs for SunSteam arrays are zero.
Client requires payback less than 5 years based on cost savings only.
Price of thermal coal varies; breakeven coal price for expansion is $87.50 per ton.
additional_questions: What are the potential risks and benefits of the SunSteam expansion?
What is the breakeven price of coal for the expansion project?
Given current coal prices, should the expansion be undertaken?
solution: The expansion requires $14M investment and saves 32,000 tons of coal annually. The breakeven coal price is $87.50/ton. Current coal price (~$83) is below breakeven, so payback is not currently possible. However, if coal prices rise above $87.50, payback criteria will be met. Risks include coal price volatility and technical difficulties.
evaluation: Candidate should identify qualitative risks and benefits, calculate breakeven coal price correctly, interpret coal price trends, and make a recommendation aligned with payback criteria.

title: Grad-U-Date Monetization Strategy
description: Grad-U-Date is an online dating startup for graduate students in the U.S. The app is built and ready. What is the best monetization strategy for Grad-U-Date and how profitable will the company be?
additional_info: Gender ratio is 50/50; only one revenue model can be used.
Four major competitors exist; no other graduate student-only dating apps.
Market size estimated at 6 million graduate students.
Revenue models include monthly fee, advertising, and premium accounts.
Fixed costs $10M/year; variable cost $1 per member per year.
Exhibits provide usage forecasts, revenue per user, and market trends.
additional_questions: What monetization models could Grad-U-Date use? Advantages and disadvantages?
What is the potential market size?
Calculate profit for each revenue model and recommend one.
Does additional market trend information change your recommendation?
Why did monthly fee paying users drop starting in 2013?
What other strategies can increase profit given your recommended model?
solution: The premium account model is recommended, expected to generate $20.6M annual profit. Monthly fee model is declining rapidly and limits user base. Advertising model is less profitable. Market size is 6 million graduate students. Trends show decreasing willingness to pay monthly fees due to competition and free apps like Tinder.
evaluation: Candidate should brainstorm multiple monetization models, perform accurate market sizing and profitability calculations, interpret market trends, and propose strategic profit improvement ideas.

title: A Sticky Paper Situation
description: PaperCo manufactures specialty self-adhesive papers for commercial printers in the US. The business is profitable but stagnant. The client wants to invest to restore growth. Should PaperCo expand production capacity targeting medium or large printer segments?
additional_info: PaperCo has 30% market share in small printers, 10% in medium and large segments.
Expansion can increase sales to medium or large segments by 20%.
Expansion requires fixed costs: $675K for medium segment (carton packaging), $1.3M for large segment (pallet packaging).
Unit prices and costs vary by segment; profit per unit: small $10, medium $9, large $7.
Annual usage per customer: small 100 units, medium 500 units, large 3,000 units.
additional_questions: What is the current profitability by segment?
What is the ROI of expanding production in medium vs large segments?
Which segment should PaperCo invest in and why?
solution: Medium segment expansion yields a higher 1-year ROI (3.0) compared to large segment (2.23) due to lower fixed costs despite smaller absolute profit increase. Recommendation is to expand production in medium printer segment first, with potential large segment expansion later.
evaluation: Candidate should calculate segment profitability, incremental profit from expansion, ROI, and make a clear investment recommendation with rationale.

title: BankCoCard Online Bank Launch
description: BankCoCard, a US credit card subsidiary, wants to lower cost of funds by launching an online bank. They must choose to launch either a savings or checking account this year (the other next year). Should they launch savings or checking accounts first?
additional_info: Both products cost $10M to build and take same time.
Online bank operates only in US.
Current cost of funds is 4%; online bank will lend deposits to credit card portfolio at 3%.
Savings account: average deposit $5,000, 5-year life, 2% interest paid to customer.
Checking account: average deposit $2,000, 10-year life, 0% interest paid, overdraft fees apply.
Market sizes: 20M savings accounts, 15M checking accounts; expect 10% market capture first year.
additional_questions: What upfront and ongoing costs and revenues are associated with each product?
What are possible customer acquisition strategies?
Calculate lifetime profit per account for savings vs checking.
Calculate total lifetime profit for accounts opened in first year.
Are there any suspicious assumptions in the data?
solution: Checking accounts generate higher lifetime profit per account ($280 vs $110) and total profit ($420M vs $220M) despite smaller market size. Recommendation is to launch checking accounts first to maximize profit and reduce cost of funds.
evaluation: Candidate should identify cost/revenue drivers, perform lifetime profit calculations, analyze market sizes, question assumptions, and recommend product launch order.

title: GGC Health Revenue Growth
description: GGC Health operates eight Ambulatory Surgical Centers (ASCs) with flat revenues of $400M/year. The CEO wants to increase revenues by 15%. Which city should GGC Health enter for a new ASC and how else can revenue be increased?
additional_info: Three potential cities: Charlotte (30 doctors), Indianapolis (25), Nashville (16).
Physician specialty breakdown varies by city.
Patients per doctor per month: Ortho 15, Neuro 10, Gastro 12.
Reimbursement rates per patient vary by specialty.
Adding 2 urology doctors per existing ASC is possible; urology patients per doctor per month: 8; reimbursement $8,000 per patient.
Revenue target increase is $60M.
additional_questions: Which city should GGC Health enter based on doctor specialties and patient volumes?
Calculate expected revenue from new ASC in chosen city.
Brainstorm ways to increase revenue at existing ASCs.
Calculate additional revenue from adding urology doctors to existing ASCs.
solution: Indianapolis is the best city to enter due to higher revenue potential. Opening an ASC there plus adding urology doctors to existing ASCs can meet the $60M revenue growth target. Other revenue growth ideas include increasing patients per doctor, adding new procedures, and prioritizing high reimbursement surgeries.
evaluation: Candidate should analyze physician and patient data, calculate revenues accurately, brainstorm revenue growth strategies, and synthesize a comprehensive recommendation.

title: Cosmetics Company Turnaround
description: The North American CEO of a global personal and home care products company has hired Capgemini Consulting to turn around the North American business from loss to profitability within two years. The project team is stranded and you must present the turnaround plan to the board. What should be your approach?
additional_info: Internal analysis includes revenue streams, cost drivers, supply chain, channel strategy, financials, org structure, and core competencies.
Industry analysis includes competitors, rivalry intensity, retail channels, and trends.
Customer analysis includes consumer segments, sizes, saturation, and changing tastes.
solution: The candidate should analyze internal and external factors, identify cost reduction and revenue growth opportunities, assess competitive positioning, and propose strategic initiatives to restore profitability within two years.
evaluation: Candidate should demonstrate structured problem solving, integrate multiple analyses, prioritize key issues, and communicate a clear turnaround strategy.

title: Consumer Skin Care Market Entry
description: Our client is a $5 Billion private European manufacturer of medicinal products. They currently own 10% of the European market and want to double their top line within 5 years. They are considering entering the consumer skin care market, a $30B global market with fragmented competition and two major channels: Physician prescriptions and Over-the-Counter (OTC). How should they proceed with this decision?
additional_info: The client licenses new medicines and sells through traditional European wholesale distributors and direct hospital contracts.
Existing manufacturing footprint is in urban locations with higher overhead costs but commands a price premium due to quality, service, and speed.
Consumer skin care market includes products treating acne, hair loss, wrinkles, infections, fungus, psoriasis, and oily skin.
80% of products are lotions, ointments, and creams.
Pricing pressure exists due to convergence of OTC and prescription products in a low-margin business.
Global skin care market is $30B with 7% CAGR; Europe is 20% of the market with 5% growth.
Channels: Prescription (smaller, slower growth) and OTC (larger, faster growth).
Branded vs Generic products have different bases of competition and margins.
Europe is a smaller, slower growth market compared to the US and Emerging markets.
Client’s goal is to double revenue from $5B to $10B in 5 years.
additional_questions: What factors should they consider in deciding whether to enter the European Consumer Skin Care market?

Based on the client background and Data Sheet provided, how would you advise the client in developing a European channel strategy (Prescription, OTC, or Both?) and product strategy (Branded or Generic)?

Given the client’s current capabilities and aggressive growth target, if they decide to enter this market, what factors should drive their decision to explore other Geographic markets versus staying in Europe?
With the limited information and data available about the client and the consumer skin care industry, do you think expansion into this market is a good idea?
solution: The client should evaluate market attractiveness including size, growth, fragmentation, customer options, and regulatory environment. They must assess fit with their capabilities, manufacturing costs, and strategic goals. OTC channel offers higher growth but lower margins; branded prescription fits current capabilities but is smaller and slower growing. Europe alone is unlikely to meet growth targets; global expansion or alternative growth options should be considered. The client should weigh pros and cons of entering a low-margin, fragmented market versus their current premium, higher-margin business. A balanced recommendation would consider both strategic fit and financial implications.
evaluation: Candidate should demonstrate understanding of market attractiveness, strategic fit, and financial trade-offs. They should analyze channels and product strategies considering client strengths and weaknesses. Ability to synthesize data and make a clear recommendation is key.

title: Great Burger Acquisition of Heavenly Donuts
description: Great Burger (GB), the fourth largest fast food chain worldwide, is considering acquiring Heavenly Donuts (HD), a growing doughnut chain. GB operates mostly via franchising and competes with major fast food chains. HD also franchises but with a different model. GB’s CEO has hired McKinsey to advise on whether to acquire HD.
additional_info: GB offers breakfast, lunch, and dinner combos; 85% of stores are franchised.
HD franchises areas/regions requiring franchisees to open a certain number of stores.
GB has 5,000 stores globally; HD has 1,020 stores.
GB’s sales/store is $1.1M; HD’s is $0.7M; industry averages are $0.9M and $0.8M respectively.
GB’s profit margin is 6.3%; HD’s is 4.9%.
GB’s cost of sales is 51%; HD’s is 40%.
GB’s corporate G&A is 8%; HD’s is 15%.
Doughnut consumption per capita in US is $10/year, projected to double in 5 years.
GB’s CEO wants to understand potential synergies, profitability impact, and integration challenges.
additional_questions: What areas would you want to explore to determine whether GB should acquire HD?
What potential synergies can you think of between GB and HD?

The team thinks that with synergies, it should be possible to double HD’s US market share in the next 5 years, and that GB’s access to capital will allow it to expand number of HD stores by 2.5 times. What sales/store will HD require in 5 years in order for GB to achieve these goals?

One of the synergies that the team thinks might have a big potential is the idea of increasing the businesses’ overall profitability by selling doughnuts in GB stores. How would you assess the profitability impact of this synergy?

What would be the incremental profit per store if we think we are going to sell 50 thousand doughnuts per store at a price of $2 per doughnut at a 60% margin with a cannibalization rate of 10% of GB’s sales?
You run into the CEO of GB in the hall. He asks you to summarize McKinsey’s perspective so far on whether GB should acquire HD. Pretend I am the CEO - What would you say?
solution: Candidate should analyze HD’s standalone value including market growth, sales trends, competition, profitability, and capital needs. They should assess management and cultural fit, and evaluate strategic synergies such as cost savings in SG&A, purchasing, property costs, revenue synergies from cross-selling, and expansion capabilities. Quantitative analysis shows HD needs to increase sales/store to $1.2M to meet growth targets. Profitability impact of selling doughnuts in GB stores should consider incremental revenues, costs, investments, and cannibalization effects. Incremental profit per store is estimated at $5K. Overall, acquisition is likely to create significant value if synergies are realized and integration is managed well.
evaluation: Candidate should cover standalone business analysis, synergy identification, quantitative reasoning, and integration challenges. Ability to synthesize findings into a clear recommendation and communicate effectively to CEO is critical.

title: Drinks Gone Flat
description: The beverage department of a regional supermarket has seen a drop in revenue over the last year and has hired your firm to determine the cause of the decline and recommend ways to reverse the trend.
additional_info: Client is a leading regional grocer within the southeastern U.S.
Competitive landscape has not changed in the last year
Revenue decline is specifically within the beverage segment
Client wants to find the cause of the declining revenue and recommendations for how to stop the decline
Exhibit 1: Revenue / sales numbers segmented by beverage product category (provide when asked)
Exhibit 2: Revenue segmented by soda brand (provide after interviewee determines revenues are only declining in sodas and asks for further segmented information)
additional_questions: The client would like to hear your recommendations for how to reverse the decline in revenue of the beverage segment.
solution: The decline in beverage revenue is due to a drop in soda sales, specifically Brand A's volume and revenue decline while the Value Brand's volume increased but at a lower price. Recommendations include adjusting the price of the Value Brand, improving differentiation between Brand A and the Value Brand, exploring pricing strategies, bundling opportunities, marketing activities, and shelf placement changes.
evaluation: Candidate should structure the problem focusing on revenue levers (price and quantity), segment the revenue decline properly, use exhibits effectively, and provide structured, creative recommendations organized around the 4P's (Price, Promotion, Product, Placement).

title: Made to Measure
description: Our client owns a designer shoe-store that makes high end made-to-measure shoes for customers. The high-quality product has gained popularity rapidly and the client wants to ramp up production. However, the highly-skilled labor that our client employs is hard to come by. Our client wants us to help them figure out how to increase output without adversely affecting quality and the company’s potential profitability.
additional_info: There are no additional skilled shoemakers available in the market
The shoe-making process has 3 stages: cutting, stitching and finishing
Currently there are 15 workers, each of whom take an average of 12 hours to produce one pair of shoes
Each shoemaker performs all three steps of the process under the current system
The process can be changed so shoemakers perform specific tasks
Employee survey data shows hours per activity by group
Employees work 9 hours a day, 5 days a week
Price per pair: $300
Material cost: $40 per pair
Shipping cost: $10 per pair
Employee wages: $15/hour
Fixed costs: $1000/week
solution: By reorganizing the process so that each group specializes in the task they perform fastest, the average time per pair reduces from 12 hours to 9 hours, increasing weekly output from 56 to 75 pairs. This increases weekly profit to $7,625 and annual profit to $396,500. The recommendation is to implement specialization by task groups: Group 1 cutting, Group 2 finishing, Group 3 stitching.
evaluation: Candidate should identify the misalignment of skills and tasks, propose specialization to reduce production time, calculate new output and profitability correctly, and consider implementation risks and next steps.

title: Vibrants
description: Your client, a national drug-store, is adding a new type of hair dye to its retail offering. “Highlights Dye” allows customers to comb in small amounts of various color to hair rather than the traditional method of dying the whole head. The client is considering launching its own in-house brand or carrying an existing recognized brand-name – “Vibrants”. Should the drugstore carry Vibrants over the in-store brand?
additional_info: The retail chain already carries their in-house brand in many products including traditional hair dye
“Highlights Dye” is a fashion-forward product available in natural and fluorescent colors, lasting roughly 30 days per application
Target customer is under 30 years in age
Business objective is profitability
Timeline to launch is 90 days
Pricing considerations show in-house brand has superior margins but Vibrants is preferred by target customers
Customer preference data shows under 30 customers overwhelmingly prefer Vibrants
solution: The drug-store should carry Vibrants despite the in-house brand having superior margins because the target customer segment (under 30) prefers Vibrants. The 90-day launch timeline and complexity of launching a new brand support this recommendation.
evaluation: Candidate should analyze customer preferences, profitability, launch timeline, and product differentiation. They should balance margin considerations with customer demand and time to market.

title: Sports Bar Profitability and Investment Analysis
description: Your client estimates that the start-up capital needed to open the Sports Bar (e.g., renovations, equipment, licenses) is $500K. How long will it take for the business to break even? Over a 5-year period, what would be the return on this investment? (Assume no interest)
additional_info: The client’s father would have to compare this opportunity against other potential investments
4.1% return at the end of 5 years
Over the 5-year period, less than 1% return per year
Although calculations indicate that the business can be profitable, the margin is rather small relative to the initial investment required. Further, all costs may not be accounted for.
Given these results, the client’s father could likely find another investment opportunity with a faster payback period and higher ROI
Business is not projected to lose money but cost assumptions may be incomplete
Weekly revenue, COGS, labor, and lease costs are provided
Weekly and annual profit calculations are provided
solution: Break-even point is 4.8 years. The 5-year ROI is 4.1%. The business is profitable but the margin is small relative to the initial investment. The client’s father should consider other investment opportunities with faster payback and higher ROI. Additional costs such as utilities, marketing, and location risks should be considered. Recommendations include exploring additional revenue sources and cost reductions.
evaluation: Candidate should remain structured throughout calculations, verbalize each step, and consider all costs. They should recognize the small margin relative to investment and discuss risks and recommendations to improve profitability or shorten break-even.

title: The Pricing Games
description: Your client is Next Level Gaming (NLG), a start-up in the E-sports and computer gaming industry. NLG is planning to launch its first game and is considering 3 business models: subscription, retail, and free-to-play. How would you advise NLG to proceed?
additional_info: NLG is launching immediately in the US only
Competition: 3 major players with 35%, 25%, and 20% market share
Target market: players aged 21 to 40
Game compatible with Apple and Windows
R&D cost of $10M is sunk and should not be factored into decision
Exhibits provide data on market size, revenues, costs, and customer survey responses
Subscription model: monthly fee; Retail model: full price upfront; Free-to-play: free game with in-game purchases
additional_questions: What factors would you consider to estimate the number of players NLG can target with its first game? How large is this player base in the US?
What is NLG’s expected first year profitability with each of the 3 pricing models?
What non-financial factors would you consider in the subscription vs. free-to-play business models?
How does the qualitative survey information impact your outlook on the 3 different business models? What is your final recommendation?
solution: Market size estimated around 10 million players. Profitability calculations show subscription and free-to-play models yield similar profits (~$20M), retail less ($9M). Qualitative factors favor free-to-play due to larger player base, growth potential, and competitive positioning. Recommendation is to pursue free-to-play with strategies to increase paying player percentage and revenue per player. Risks include dependence on paying customers and competition.
evaluation: Candidate should structure analysis covering market sizing, profitability drivers, and qualitative factors. They should calculate profit margins, interpret survey data, and provide a consistent recommendation with risk mitigation strategies.

title: Men’s Extra Comfortable Essentials Growth Strategy
description: Our client, Men’s Extra Comfortable Essentials, is a US-based manufacturer of basic men’s apparel. Revenues in 2016 were $60M, and the CEO has promised 4x growth by 2020 with a 10.5% profit margin. Are these targets realistic, and how can the firm achieve them?
additional_info: Firm purchases fabrics via contracts and processes apparel in-house
Sells through traditional channels
Current product mix and market shares by product line provided
Market CAGR and estimated 2020 market shares provided
Gross margins by product line provided
Candidate can request data on revenue, market size, margins, and growth rates
additional_questions: Given the data, what will be our client’s 2020 revenue by product type?
Given the gross margin information, what is the firm’s gross margin for 2020?
How can the firm reach the targets promised to shareholders?
What are some ways to increase market share of the underwear product line?
solution: 2020 revenue estimated at $362.5M, exceeding CEO’s $240M target. Gross margin calculated at 7.1%, below the 10.5% target. Recommendation is to drop unprofitable Tanks and Tees lines to improve margin while maintaining revenue above target. To increase underwear market share, firm should consider segmentation, new product lines (including women’s), marketing, new distribution channels, e-commerce, and operational improvements.
evaluation: Candidate should demonstrate structured math skills, identify unprofitable lines, and propose actionable growth strategies. They should balance revenue growth with margin improvement and consider operational and marketing levers.

title: WiFi in the Sky
description: Your firm is advising a domestic airline carrier on their in-flight connectivity (IFC) strategy. With 80% of US aircraft already outfitted with IFC and rising competitive pressure, your client needs to assess their go-to-market strategy for IFC. What key factors should they consider?
additional_info: Airline flies domestic US routes plus select Canada, Mexico, Caribbean flights
IFC includes WiFi only; in-flight entertainment is separate but can be integrated
Airline has 90 planes and ~30K flights per year
IFC typically installed and managed by outside vendors with long contracts
Different vendors use different technologies with varying quality
Bandwidth is shared among passengers; pricing can affect usage and speed
additional_questions: What are your thoughts on the two competitor IFC business models shown in Exhibit 1?
If the airline partners with a white-label IFC vendor, what percentage of passengers need to purchase a session to break even in 2 years? (Use Exhibit 2)
Take rates are currently 5-10%. What are key drivers of IFC take rate?
What is your recommendation for the airline’s IFC strategy?
solution: Competitor 1’s vendor-branded, revenue-share model has less operational burden but less pricing control; Competitor 2’s airline-branded, wholesale model offers more control but higher costs. Break-even take rate is 12.9%, higher than current 5-10% take rates, indicating risk. Key take rate drivers include flight length, price, service quality, passenger demographics. Recommendation is to partner with a white-label vendor to retain pricing and user experience control, roll out gradually prioritizing high take-rate routes, and pilot test pricing and experience.
evaluation: Candidate should analyze pros and cons of business models, perform break-even math accurately, brainstorm take rate drivers, and provide a thoughtful recommendation considering risks and operational factors.

title: MJ Wineries Profitability Improvement
description: MJ Wineries produces award-winning Chardonnay in Sonoma Valley but has seen declining profitability. The CEO wants recommendations to immediately improve profitability. What could be impacting profitability and what should MJ Wineries do?
additional_info: Wine production takes at least 9 months; wines improve with aging
MJ Wineries owns a 3,000-acre vineyard and production equipment
Industry is capital intensive with many small producers
Customer segments include liquor stores, restaurants, bars, supermarkets
US market share and profit margins by wine type provided
Customer preference shifting from white to red wine
additional_questions: What could be impacting profitability at MJ Wineries?
Which grape variety should MJ Wineries consider for producing a new wine? (Exhibits 1 & 2)
What are pros and cons of moving into the Pinot Noir market?
What other ways could MJ Wineries increase revenue without introducing a new wine?
solution: Profitability impacted by market shifts, cost increases, and competitive pressures. MJ Wineries should diversify into Pinot Noir due to growing red wine preference, suitable climate, and brand benefits. Pros include market growth and risk diversification; cons include higher capital costs and competitive challenges. Other revenue options include producing other alcohol types, expanding winery services (tours, weddings), selling land, aging wine longer, or partnering with wine critics.
evaluation: Candidate should brainstorm broadly on profitability drivers, analyze market trends, assess diversification pros and cons, and propose creative revenue growth ideas beyond product introduction.

title: Botox Migraine Market Entry
description: The maker of Botox is considering expanding into the migraine market with a new product expected to receive FDA approval in 2019. How will you determine if the market is attractive? If attractive, what recommendations do you have for launch? What concerns should the client consider before launching?
additional_info: Drug priced at $200 per injection, given every 2 months (6 injections/year)
Remaining development and launch costs approx. $5B; marketing expense $1B annually
Manufacturing marginal costs are low
Product offers similar efficacy to market leader but without side effects and has added skin care benefits
Market leader lost patent protection; strong OTC generic market exists
US population approx. 320M; 15% suffer chronic migraines; 25% of those have severe migraines
Expected market share for Botox is 30% within 5 years
additional_questions: What concerns should the client take into consideration before deciding to launch?
solution: Estimated total market size is 12M patients with potential annual revenue of $14.4B; Botox’s 30% share yields $4.32B. Concerns include brand image risk due to Botox’s cosmetic association, risk exposure if side effects arise, competition from generics, high development and marketing costs, and market acceptance. Recommendations include careful branding strategy, pricing, and risk mitigation before launch.
evaluation: Candidate should perform structured market sizing, assess launch considerations including costs, competition, branding, and risks, and provide a balanced recommendation.

title: Bike Helmets
description: Your client is a private equity firm considering purchasing a firm that makes bicycle helmets. You have been hired to help advise whether or not to make the acquisition.
additional_info: The company only sells its products in the U.S.
The company currently has an estimated market share of 60% of helmets sold in the U.S.
The company has had a string of ineffective CEOs
The company’s helmets have an average price of $30
Helmet prices are not expected to change in the future.
The market is mature with few new entrants
U.S. regulations require all riders under the age of 18 to wear a helmet
PE firm holds companies for an average of 7 years
PE firm has a return target of 10%/year
PE firm also owns a bicycle manufacturer (possible synergies unknown)
Recent sales of similar manufacturing companies data available
Market sizing assumptions: US population 320M, 50% bike riders, 50% wear helmets, replacement every 2 years
solution: Candidate should estimate market size and value the company using comparable sales multiples. They should consider revenue, costs, growth, and synergies with existing portfolio companies. The recommendation depends on whether the expected returns meet the PE firm's target of 10% annualized return over a typical holding period. Risks include mature market and low growth; opportunities include improving management and synergies.
evaluation: Candidate should demonstrate structured thinking, ability to size market, apply valuation methods, consider synergies, and make a clear recommendation with supporting rationale. They should justify assumptions and consider risks and benefits.

title: Sunshine Power
description: You are a consultant who has been engaged by Sunshine Power, owner of a coal power station on the Sunshine Coast of Australia. They are considering expanding a solar technology pilot project (SunSteam arrays) that reduces coal consumption. Should they accept the proposal to build four additional arrays?
additional_info: Cost of one SunSteam array assumed $3.5M AUD
One array saves 0.2% of annual coal consumption
Plant consumes 4 million metric tons of coal annually
SunSteam operating costs assumed zero
Client requires payback less than 5 years based on internal cost savings
Price of thermal coal over last 5 years provided
Pilot project broke even after 6 years
No discount rate assumed
additional_questions: Assess potential risks and benefits of the SunSteam expansion qualitatively.
Calculate the breakeven price of coal for the expansion project.
Based on coal price trends, should the client proceed with the expansion?
solution: The breakeven coal price is $87.50 per ton. Current coal price (~$83) is below breakeven, so payback is not currently achievable. However, if coal prices rise above $87.50, payback criteria will be met. Risks include coal price volatility and technical difficulties. Benefits include reduced coal consumption, potential carbon credits, and CSR advantages. Recommendation depends on coal price outlook and ability to mitigate risks.
evaluation: Candidate should structure qualitative and quantitative analysis, calculate breakeven price correctly, identify risks and benefits, and provide a reasoned recommendation. They should demonstrate understanding of investment criteria and external factors.

title: Grad-U-Date
description: Our client is an online dating startup named Grad-U-Date, specializing in matching graduate students. They have finished building their app and seek advice on the best monetization strategy and profitability.
additional_info: Gender ratio 50/50
MBA, law, and medical students considered professional students
App is a sunk cost
Operating only in the US
4 major competitors and many small competitors
Significant increase in free dating apps since late 2012
No other dating service focuses only on graduate students
Only one revenue model can be used
Fixed costs $10M/year, variable cost $1 per member per year
Market size data and monetization model usage percentages provided
Historical data on paying users and fees provided
additional_questions: Does the historical data change your recommendation for monetization model?
Why did the percentage of users paying monthly fees drop starting in 2013?
What other strategies can Grad-U-Date use to increase profit given your recommended revenue model?
solution: Candidate should brainstorm multiple monetization models (monthly fee, premium account, advertising, etc.), calculate profits for each, and recommend the premium account model as it yields the highest profit ($20.6M). They should recognize the decline in monthly fee users and consider strategies to increase profit such as cost reduction, new services, and market expansion.
evaluation: Candidate should demonstrate structured brainstorming, accurate market sizing, profitability calculations, and strategic thinking about market trends and growth opportunities. They should justify their monetization recommendation and address risks.

title: GGC Health
description: Our client, GGC Health, operates eight Ambulatory Surgical Centers (ASCs) on the east coast. Revenues have been flat at $400M/year. The CEO wants to increase revenues by 15%. How should they achieve this?
additional_info: ASCs provide same-day surgical care
Physicians can perform surgeries at hospitals or ASCs and generally dictate location
Target revenue increase is $60M
Revenue = number of procedures * reimbursement per procedure
Three potential new cities for expansion: Charlotte (30 doctors), Indianapolis (25), Nashville (16)
Physician specialty breakdown and reimbursement rates provided
Orthopedic: 15 patients/doctor/month, $5K reimbursement
Neurology: 10 patients/doctor/month, $25K reimbursement
Gastroenterology: 12 patients/doctor/month, $15K reimbursement
Potential to add 2 urology doctors per existing ASC (8 sites), each with 8 patients/month, $8K reimbursement
additional_questions: Which city should GGC Health enter for a new ASC?
Calculate revenue potential per city based on doctors and specialties.
Brainstorm ways to increase revenue at existing ASCs to meet the target.
Calculate additional revenue from adding urology doctors to existing ASCs.
solution: Indianapolis is the best city to enter based on doctor specialties and revenue potential but alone does not meet the $60M target. Adding urology doctors to existing ASCs can generate an additional $12M. Combined, these actions meet the revenue growth goal. Candidate should also brainstorm other revenue growth strategies at existing sites.
evaluation: Candidate should analyze data accurately, prioritize expansion options, perform revenue calculations, and creatively brainstorm growth strategies. They should integrate quantitative and qualitative insights to recommend a comprehensive plan.

title: Skin Care Market
description: Our client is a $5B European manufacturer of medicinal products considering entering the European consumer skin care market, a $30B global market. They want to double their top line in 5 years. Should they enter this market and how?
additional_info: Client currently owns 10% of European medicinal products market
Manufacturing footprint in urban locations with higher overhead costs
Client commands price premium due to quality, service, speed
Consumer skin care market is fragmented, growing at 7% CAGR globally
Market segments: lotions, ointments, creams (80% of products)
Two major channels: Physician prescriptions (pharmacies) and Over-the-Counter (retail)
Pricing pressure on prescription products due to OTC convergence
Data on market size, growth rates, geographic splits, and basis of competition provided
additional_questions: What factors should be considered in deciding whether to enter the European consumer skin care market?
Based on data, advise on channel strategy (Prescription, OTC, or Both) and product strategy (Branded or Generic).
What factors should drive decision to explore other geographic markets versus staying in Europe?
Given limited data, is expansion into this market a good idea?
solution: Candidate should analyze market attractiveness, fit with client capabilities, growth potential, margin trade-offs, and strategic fit. OTC channel may offer better growth but lower margins. Europe is a smaller, slower growth market compared to US and emerging markets. Entry into skin care may help top-line growth but could reduce margins. Recommendation depends on weighing these factors and alternative growth options.
evaluation: Candidate should demonstrate ability to analyze market data, consider strategic fit, evaluate channels and products, and make a reasoned recommendation. They should weigh pros and cons and consider alternative strategies.

title: Chococo
description: Chococo is a premium chocolate company based in Peru looking to expand into the United States. What should they consider before launching this expansion?
additional_info: Chococo currently operates only in Peru and sells chocolate in stores
Annual revenue $100M
Premium chocolate industry growing >10% annually
Pricing similar to competitors
US population and chocolate purchasing behavior data provided
Candidate should estimate US premium chocolate market size
Candidate should identify risks of entering US market
Candidate should identify and prioritize channels of entry
additional_questions: What is the size of the premium chocolate industry in the United States?
What are some risks associated with entering the US market?
Through what channels should Chococo enter the US market?
solution: Candidate should estimate US premium chocolate market size (~$4B), assess feasibility of capturing ~3% market share to reach $100M revenue, identify risks such as competition, brand awareness, regulatory hurdles, production capacity, and financial risks, and recommend entry channels prioritized by ease, financial return, and customer reach (e.g., online, retail stores, partnerships).
evaluation: Candidate should demonstrate market sizing skills, risk assessment, and strategic channel selection. They should prioritize and justify recommendations clearly.

title: Adventure Capital
description: Your client, Idaho Johnson, an archeologist/adventurer, is considering launching an expedition to recover a valuable 6,000 year old crown buried in Egypt. Should he proceed with the expedition?
additional_info: Client must self-finance the dig but has a buyer lined up pending recovery
Colleague offers to sell a map to the site
Client is located in Indiana
Expedition estimated to take 12 months
Client wants an expected ROI of 85%
Chance of success estimated at 20%
Client has some equipment but needs to buy more
additional_questions: What costs would be required to embark on this expedition?
Calculate the total cost of the expedition.
Estimate expected returns and decide whether to proceed.
solution: Candidate should identify upfront costs (map, travel, permits, equipment), recurring costs (labor, lodging, food), and opportunity costs (teaching salary). Using provided cost data, candidate should calculate total costs, expected value of artifact sale considering 20% success chance, and compare to required ROI. Recommendation depends on whether expected ROI meets 85% threshold.
evaluation: Candidate should demonstrate structured cost identification, quantitative calculation, risk assessment, and decision-making under uncertainty.

title: Chemical Consolidation: Question 2
description: The company is attempting to identify major buckets of potential synergies and has asked for your help in laying out potential areas to focus on in realizing synergies from the combination. How should the company think about this?
